name: United States
culture_notes: Visual Logic: High-contrast, cinematic lighting. If it's a casual game, use vibrant, saturated colors; if hardcore, use 'Gritty' and 'Realistic' textures.
Core Narrative: The 'Underdog Story'. From a prisoner/beggar to a King/CEO. Americans love the 'Zero to Hero' trajectory.
Decision Making: Feature 'Choice-based' UI (Option A or B). Even if the choice is fake, it triggers the American user's desire for 'Freedom' and 'Control'.
Humor: Use Sarcasm, Slapstick, or 'Epic Fails'. Self-deprecating humor in UGC (User Generated Content) style ads performs exceptionally well.
Social Proof: Influencer endorsements (even micro-influencers) or 'Reaction' face-cams are more trusted than official studio trailers.
creative_hooks: The 'Logic Challenge': 'Only 1% can solve this' or 'Is your IQ 140?'. It triggers their competitive nature.
The 'Power Trip': Rapidly increasing numbers (EXP, Gold, Level) filling the screen with explosive VFX.
The 'Justice/Revenge' Hook: Start with the protagonist being cheated on or bullied, then immediate revenge through gameplay.
UGC/TikTok Style: A real person holding the phone, talking directly to the camera: 'Stop playing those fake games, this one is actually real.'
preferred_bgm: Deep Bass, Cinematic Orchestral (for SLG/RPG) or Upbeat Hip-Hop/Phonk (for Action/Casual).
focus: Instant Gratification (Action) > Strategic Freedom (Control) > Social Status (Competition)
taboo: General extreme violence or severe local offenses
